steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast: Hawaiian literature is highly heterogeneous in many ways, yet it is also characterized by thematic ______. The colon introduces evidence — scholars have linked contemporary writers to traditional Kanaka Maoli stories. The blank needs a word that captures the idea of themes persisting across time from tradition to the present.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘innovation’: Innovation means newness or departure from tradition. The passage describes connections to tradition, not breaks from it, so this is the opposite of what the evidence supports. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘continuity’: Continuity means themes carrying forward unbroken from traditional stories to contemporary writers — exactly what the scholars' connections demonstrate. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘subtlety’: Subtlety describes a quality of how something is expressed (delicately, not obviously), not a relationship between past and present traditions. Nothing in the evidence points to themes being subtle. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘sophistication’: Sophistication describes complexity or refinement, not the persistence of thematic elements across time. The evidence about linked works does not imply sophistication. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
